perturbation.original_text: Endorser's endorsements of the Products and Licensed Products will be in accordance with the guidelines established by the Federal Trade Commission for endorsements in advertising. If requested by MusclePharm, Endorser shall provide a signed affidavit in form satisfactory to MusclePharm confirming Endorser's compliance with the FTC standards in connection with his endorsements and endorsement activities.
perturbation.changed_text: Endorser's endorsements of the Products and Licensed Products should generally align with the guidelines referenced by the Federal Trade Commission for endorsements in advertising. If requested by MusclePharm, Endorser may provide a signed affidavit in a form that Endorser deems reasonable confirming Endorser's general awareness of FTC standards.
perturbation.explanation: This modification weakens the obligation to comply with FTC guidelines. Replacing 'will be in accordance with' with 'should generally align with' makes adherence discretionary. Changing 'shall provide a signed affidavit in form satisfactory to MusclePharm' to 'may provide a signed affidavit in a form that Endorser deems reasonable' gives the endorser control over the affidavit's form and whether to provide it, and replaces 'compliance' with 'general awareness'.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 15 U.S. Code § 45 - Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission
perturbation.law_explanation: The FTC Act prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices in commerce, and its endorsement guidelines require that endorsements be truthful and not misleading. This change contradicts that law by creating ambiguity around the obligation to adhere to those guidelines.

Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission (a) Declaration of unlawfulness; power to prohibit unfair practices; inapplicability to foreign trade (1) Unfair methods of competition i
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text weakens the requirement to comply with FTC guidelines, creating a legal contradiction. The original text states endorsements 'will be in accordance' with FTC guidelines, while the changed text states they 'should generally align', making compliance discretionary. The original text requires an affidavit 'in form satisfactory to MusclePharm', while the changed text allows an affidavit in a form that the 'Endorser deems reasonable', weakening MusclePharm's control and allowing the endorser to provide an affidavit only confirming 'general awareness' of FTC standards rather than 'compliance'. 15 USC 45 states 'Unfair methods of competition unlawful; prevention by Commission', and the FTC Act prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices, including endorsements, thus, creating ambiguity around the obligation to adhere to those guidelines contradicts that law.

perturbation.original_text: MusclePharm shall utilize its commercially reasonable efforts to advertise and promote the Licensed Products at its own expense and to sell the Licensed Products through the Distribution Channels worldwide as contemplated herein during the Term and to promote both the goodwill of the Endorser and the market reputation of the Licensed Products. MusclePharm will conduct its activities relating to the marketing of the Licensed Products in a professional manner.
perturbation.changed_text: MusclePharm will endeavor to advertise and promote the Licensed Products at its own expense and to sell the Licensed Products through the Distribution Channels worldwide as contemplated herein during the Term, and will aim to promote both the goodwill of the Endorser and the market reputation of the Licensed Products. MusclePharm will generally conduct its activities relating to the marketing of the Licensed Products.
perturbation.explanation: The modification replaces 'shall utilize its commercially reasonable efforts' with 'will endeavor', decreasing the level of effort required. The phrase 'will aim to promote' replaces the original 'to promote,' suggesting intention rather than an obligation. Also, removing "in a professional manner" from the sentence "MusclePharm will conduct its activities relating to the marketing of the Licensed Products in a professional manner" weakens the language and reduces the performance standard.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code § 17500 prohibits false or misleading advertising. By reducing the standard of effort and introducing vagueness, this modified text makes it more difficult to enforce the promise of advertising in a way that is not misleading, potentially contradicting the law.

It is unlawful for any person, firm, corporation or association, or any employee thereof with intent directly or indirectly to dispose of real or personal property or to perform services, professional or otherwise, or anything of any nature whatsoever or to induce the public to enter into any obligation relating thereto, to make or disseminate or cause to be made or disseminated before the public in this state, or to make or disseminate or cause to be made or disseminated from this state before the public in any state, in any newspaper or other publication, or any advertising device, or by public outcry or proclamation, or in any other manner or means whatever, including over the Internet, any statement, concerning that real or personal property or those services, professional or otherwise, or concerning any circumstance or matter of fact connected with the propo
perturbation.justification: The modification weakens the obligation to advertise the licensed products effectively, potentially resulting in misleading advertising. The original text requires 'commercially reasonable efforts,' while the changed text uses 'will endeavor' and 'will aim to promote,' which are less stringent. This change introduces ambiguity regarding the actual effort required. California Business and Professions Code § 17500 states that it is unlawful to disseminate any statement concerning services 'in any manner or means whatever' that could be considered false or misleading. The reduced standard of effort in the changed text increases the risk of misleading advertising, contradicting the BPC 17500's prohibition of false advertising.

perturbation.original_text: The Licensed Products shall be of the highest quality and manufactured, produced, sold, distributed and promoted in strict compliance with all applicable laws and regulations, and be of substantially the same or better quality as the samples previously submitted by MusclePharm. MusclePharm shall be responsible for ensuring that the products are properly designed and manufactured for safe use and shall promptly and fairly address and resolve all consumer complaints and warranty claims.
perturbation.changed_text: The Licensed Products should generally be of high quality and manufactured, produced, sold, distributed and promoted with general awareness of applicable laws and regulations, and be of similar quality as the samples previously submitted by MusclePharm. MusclePharm will attempt to ensure that the products are adequately designed and manufactured for reasonably safe use and will endeavor to address consumer complaints and warranty claims.
perturbation.explanation: The modification weakens the quality and compliance standards. Replacing 'shall be of the highest quality' with 'should generally be of high quality' and 'in strict compliance' with 'with general awareness' significantly reduces the commitment. Changing 'shall be responsible for ensuring' to 'will attempt to ensure' and 'shall promptly and fairly address and resolve' to 'will endeavor to address' introduces ambiguity about MusclePharm's responsibility and the speed/fairness of resolution.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 U.S. Code § 331 prohibits the introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of any food, drug, device, or cosmetic that is adulterated or misbranded. By lowering the quality standards and compliance requirements, the changed text increases the risk that products could be adulterated or misbranded, thus violating this federal law. Specifically, it makes it more difficult to prove that MusclePharm failed to take reasonable steps to prevent the introduction of such products into commerce.

Prohibited acts The following acts and the causing thereof are prohibited: (a) The introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of any food, drug, device, tobacco product, or cosmetic that is adulterated or misbranded. (b) The adulteration or misbranding of any food, drug, device, tobacco product, or cosmetic in interstate commerce. (c) The receipt in in
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text contradicts 21 U.S. Code § 331, which prohibits introducing adulterated or misbranded products into interstate commerce. The original text mandates 'highest quality' and 'strict compliance,' while the changed text weakens these standards to 'generally be of high quality' and 'with general awareness,' increasing the risk of non-compliance. The original text requires MusclePharm to 'ensure' product safety and 'promptly and fairly address' complaints, while the changed text states they 'will attempt to ensure' and 'will endeavor to address,' introducing ambiguity and weakening the obligation. According to the snippet, §331(a) prohibits '[t]he introduction or delivery for introduction into interstate commerce of any food, drug, device, tobacco product, or cosmetic that is adulterated or misbranded.' The modifications lessen the quality and compliance standards increasing the possibility that adulterated or misbranded products are introduced into interstate commerce.
